business_plan.problem: LATAM gig platforms can't pay USD on-chain.
business_plan.icp: BR-based gig platforms with cross-border payouts.
business_plan.solution: Stablecoin payouts API on Solana with BR off-ramp.

validation_report.demand_evidence: iFood asked publicly for USD payouts.
validation_report.gap_summary: Mercado Pago has BRL but no USD rails.

voices.name: analyst
voices.position: LATAM gig payouts is a real wedge - Mercado Pago skips USD rails.
voices.tension: Pushed back on API-first - gig platforms want a dashboard, not SDK.
voices.recommendation: Ship a hosted payout console first, expose API in V2.

voices.name: critic
voices.position: Off-ramp to BRL is the real bottleneck; on-chain is the easy half.
voices.tension: Resisted shoehorning fraud-ML; called it premature optimisation.
voices.recommendation: Partner with one local off-ramp before building.

voices.name: architect
voices.position: Architecturally clean - stateless API, idempotency keys, webhooks.
voices.tension: Worried about regulatory drift across BR/MX/AR.
voices.recommendation: Launch BR-only; gate other geos on local counsel sign-off.

voices.name: scoper
voices.position: V1 fits in 4 days if scoped to one off-ramp partner.
voices.tension: Dissent on attribution: receipts must show platform, not Gecko.
voices.recommendation: White-label receipt page with platform brand, Gecko in fine print.

voices.name: judge
voices.position: SHIP V1 to BR gig platforms with a single off-ramp partner.

transcript_summary: The analyst argued LATAM gig payouts form a real wedge against Mercado Pago. The critic pushed back that the off-ramp is the bottleneck, not the rails. The judge concluded SHIP V1 with a single BR off-ramp partner. The scoper's dissent on attribution survived: payout receipts must brand the platform.

competitors.name: Mercado Pago
competitors.why_we_are_not_them: Mercado Pago settles in BRL through traditional banking rails; we settle USD on-chain with x402 receipts so platforms get instant programmable payouts.

competitors.name: Wise
competitors.why_we_are_not_them: Wise ships generic cross-border SMB payouts; our wedge is a typed, hash-addressed payout API scoped to LATAM gig platforms.

competitors.name: Transfero
competitors.why_we_are_not_them: Transfero is an off-ramp endpoint only; we orchestrate off-ramp + on-chain settlement + idempotent platform-side API in one mix.

dissents.verbatim: If the payout receipt shows Gecko's brand, we eat the platform's user trust. Attribution must default to the platform; Gecko is fine print.

surviving_dissent.rationale: Position was never refuted; judge noted as open risk.

steps.action: Sign LOI with Transfero for BR off-ramp partnership.

falsifier.what_would_disprove_this: No signed LOI with Transfero (named integration)

steps.action: Prototype hosted payout console with one design-partner gig platform.

falsifier.what_would_disprove_this: Fewer than 1 BR gig platform commits as design partner
